threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You freeze up when someone asks for directions to Dalston Junction, like it's a secret club only locals know.
Last night on the 35, a guy was arguing with his reflection in the window. Brixton at 3 AM is a whole other world.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a neighbor at the bakery who knew my coffee order by heart—it felt like home, despite the chaos around.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a U-Bahn that suddenly stops for 'technical reasons' while a karaoke party kicks off among the passengers. Pure Kreuzberg magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local when they know to avoid Civic Center at closing time—last week, I watched BART evacuate in a flash after one too many 'loud bangs.'

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Only in the Tenderloin can you see a dog lounging on the Muni tracks while a dozen commuters just shrug it off, checking their phones like it’s all normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a sandwich at any corner but miss the hidden bakeries that have fresh pa amb tomàquet just waiting to be discovered.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their hesitance at using the push buttons on the metro; locals just know they don’t always work.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'off-licence' is just a fancy word for corner shop. Good luck asking for a pint of milk there!
Heard the 59 bus driver say he's never seen so many drunk pigeons at midnight. Guess those late-night kebabs aren’t just for the humans.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a spontaneous open-air concert on a random Tuesday in the cemetery. Only in Neukölln do you turn the dead zone into a living one.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find clubs that open at dawn and serve breakfast. Nothing like a techno rave ending with eggs benedict!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always tell when someone's new if they stop at the crosswalk when the light's green. We're always dodging cars in this city.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit chasing a Muni bus today, briefcase flailing like a flag. He missed it but ended up sharing a laugh with the driver. Classic Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into a bar and order a 'mojito'. Here, a 'tinto de verano' is the way to go if you want to fit in.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled expressions at the Mercat de la Llibertat when they realize how heavy the bags of fresh produce really are.
